purpose: Define the first implementation contracts for Phase 10 adapter work. These contracts convert Phase 9 feat metadata families into runtime behavior while reusing existing engines instead of creating parallel systems.
nonNegotiableBehavior: Think Before Coding: each adapter must name the existing SSOT it extends before code is written.
Simplicity First: adapters should map metadata to existing engines; they should not become new broad subsystems unless the runtime is truly missing.
Surgical Changes: start with one adapter family at a time and keep mutation boundaries intact.
Goal-Driven Execution: every adapter must list the Phase 9 feats it unlocks and the tests/audits that prove it.
globalRules: All actor mutations must route through ActorEngine or an existing engine that delegates to ActorEngine.
All per-round/per-encounter activation limits must route through ReactionEngine, ActivationLimitEngine, EncounterUseTracker, or an existing resource service.
Reaction work must reuse ReactionEngine and ReactionRegistry; do not create a parallel reaction engine.
Damage work must reuse ActorEngine.applyDamage and DamageResolutionEngine; do not bypass the damage pipeline.
Attack option math must reuse CombatOptionResolver; do not duplicate attack/damage option math.
Skill-use feats must not alter derived skill totals unless the feat actually grants a passive bonus.
Metadata-only fields are not implementation until an adapter consumes them.

outputContract: available reaction options for chat/UI prompts
optional mutation plan routed through ActorEngine
activation usage recorded through existing limit engine
firstImplementationSlice: Add registry-backed metadata loader for simple reactionRules.
Add audit guard that reactionRules cannot be called missing_runtime while ReactionEngine exists.
Implement one non-mutating reaction prompt first, then one ActorEngine mutation reaction.
doneWhen: reactionRules metadata can produce ReactionRegistry entries without hardcoding each feat in ReactionEngine.
per-round/per-encounter limits are enforced by ActivationLimitEngine or existing reaction limit pathways.
reaction handlers never mutate actor.system directly.

outputContract: target mutation plan or chat-confirmed manual rider
chat card summary of rider application
firstImplementationSlice: Consume targetEffectsOnHit from CombatOptionResolver.collectAttackModifiers output.
Support one safe effect type first: condition-track shift on target after confirmed hit.
Gate target mutation behind explicit hit/critical result context.
doneWhen: collected targetEffectsOnHit no longer die in the roll result payload.
one target CT rider can be applied safely through ActorEngine.
unsupported target effect types produce visible chat/manual notes rather than silent failure.

inputMetadataFamilies: target has not acted timing predicates
outputContract: audit-visible reason for any damage mutation
firstImplementationSlice: Fix Advantageous Attack by replacing wrong-shape attack bonus with a post-hit damage rider predicate.
Expose targetHasActedThisEncounter in attack/damage context.
Add audit that damage riders cannot be encoded as attack bonuses.
doneWhen: Advantageous Attack can leave implemented_incorrect.
damage changes happen before or through ActorEngine.applyDamage, never direct HP mutation.
timing predicates are explicit and testable.

firstImplementationSlice: Add read-only skill-use option collector first.
Wire one non-mutating rule: allow/forbid Take 10 or Take 20 in a specific context.
Only then add roll/result mutation workflows.
doneWhen: skillUseRules has a runtime consumer.
skill-use feats no longer require fake passive skill totals.
encounter-limited skill uses use existing activation/encounter tracking.

outputContract: recipient bonus or target penalty active effect
firstImplementationSlice: Define Aid Another result context without mutating actors.
Support one simple ally attack bonus/penalty case through active effect or chat prompt.
Leave complex positioning/fear riders manual until cover/movement context exists.
doneWhen: aidAnotherRules metadata has a runtime consumer.
Aid Another bonuses are scoped to a result/context, not permanent actor math.
unsupported complex riders are surfaced visibly.

inputMetadataFamilies: scoped force power activation bonus metadata
firstImplementationSlice: Expose normalized forcePowerContext from AbilityExecutionRouter to ForceEngine.
Support scoped activation bonuses for named power matching only.
Defer post-result condition/rider effects until CombatTargetEffectAdapter is available.
doneWhen: named Forceful feats are not modeled as generic Use the Force bonuses.
force power scoped bonuses can be audited by source power name.
Force Point spends remain routed through existing force/resource services.

outputContract: manual notes for unsupported template edits
firstImplementationSlice: Build read-only normalized area/autofire context from an attack result.
Surface unsupported template edits as chat/manual prompts.
Add one per-target damage modifier case only after the context is stable.
doneWhen: area/autofire feat metadata no longer silently disappears.
per-target area modifiers can be applied before/after Evasion as specified.
cover/soft-cover provider identity is explicit in the context.
